jobs.title: Business Development Director - US
jobs.description:
About the company
Biorce is a pioneering Healthtech company dedicated to revolutionizing drug development through the power of AI. We are passionate about accelerating medical advancements and improving patient outcomes.
Our team comprises seasoned clinical research professionals, data scientists, and AI experts, working collaboratively to bridge the gap between cutting-edge technology and real-world clinical needs.
With an unwavering commitment to revolutionize healthcare, we envision a world where all patients benefit from accelerated and cost-effective access to treatments. Biorce is poised to redefine the landscape of healthcare, shaping a future where innovation and accessibility converge for the betterment of humanity.

About the role
Following our successful expansion into the U.S. and continued growth across Europe, we are seeking a Director of Clinical Solutions
to help drive our commercial function from one of our U.S. hubs ( New York or Austin ).
As our Director of Clinical Solutions, you’ll be a critical member of our commercial team, responsible for driving growth, building relationships with biopharma partners, and positioning Biorce as a category leader in AI for clinical development. This is a high-impact role that combines strategy, sales, and client relationship management, with significant opportunity for career growth as our U.S. footprint expands.
Who We're Looking For

A commercially sharp operator with deep credibility in the U.S. biopharma space. Someone who understands clinical trial processes inside out, has the network to open doors, and the drive to close them. This person should be able to work with full autonomy - owning their territory, generating their own pipeline, and representing Biorce at the highest levels of the industry.
Key Responsibilities

Own and drive the full sales cycle, from lead generation to deal closure with pharmaceutical and biotech clients across the East Coast USA.
Responsibility for hitting quarterly targets and consistently delivering against ambitious commercial goals.
Develop and execute go-to-market strategies tailored to the U.S. market, in collaboration with the Chief Commercial Officer and Global head of commercial operations.
Identify high-value potential clients and decision makers, and cultivate strong, trust based relationships.
Represent Biorce at industry events, conferences, and client meetings.
Collaborate closely with Marketing, Clinical Solutions, and Product teams to tailor pitches and ensure client success.
Provide market intelligence and feedback to inform product development and strategic decisions.
Help define and refine commercial processes and infrastructure as we scale operations in the U.S.
Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively across teams, you take full ownership of your objectives, responsibilities, and assigned territory with professionalism and drive.

Requirements

✅ Must-haves
5+ years of experience in business development, strategic partnerships, or enterprise sales in the biotech, pharmaceutical, or CRO sectors.
Deep understanding of clinical trial processes and a strong network in the U.S. biopharma ecosystem.
Proven track record of closing mid-to-large enterprise deals and building long-term relationships with C-level stakeholders.
Entrepreneurial mindset with the ability to operate independently in a fast-paced, startup environment.
Strong communication and presentation skills
Ability to explain complex technology in simple, value driven terms.
Willingness to travel up to 50% across the US and internationally to company offsites.

✨ Nice-to-Haves
Familiarity with AI-driven platforms in a clinical or drug development context

What We Offer

Opportunity to shape the future of a fast-growing AI healthtech company entering the U.S. market.
Opportunity to grow into broader strategic role as Biorce expands.
US (New York / Austin): $150,000 - $200,000

This role is commission-eligible. Final compensation depends on experience, skills, and location. The range above reflects our good-faith estimate at the time of posting, in line with Labor Law and similar pay transparency regulations.

Why Join Us?

A dynamic work environment with an international team, where collaboration and diversity thrive
Work alongside top talent, united by a shared purpose and committed to making a real impact
Comprehensive private health coverage to ensure your physical and mental well-being
Hybrid work model offering flexibility to balance your professional and personal life
Company events to celebrate achievements and enjoy time together
Get equipped with a MacBook to enhance your productivity and work experience
Our office is pet-friendly! You'll likely be greeted by a few wagging tails upon arrival

--
By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be collected, processed, and retained by the company solely for the purposes of managing and assessing my candidacy.

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
The Oregon Boating Foundation, a 501(c)(3) charitable organization located in Newport, Oregon, is looking for an Executive Director with a passion for kayaking, sailing, and outdoor education. The ideal candidate is an experienced leader with excellent verbal and written communication skills that will represent our organization publicly, support a culture of longevity, and grow the capacity of the organization.

This is a unique opportunity for the right person to advance their career, positively impact our organization on a daily basis, and grow our programs.

Job Description

The Executive Director contributes to the Oregon Boating Foundation’s mission of making safe, affordable boating opportunities available to everyone by managing the day-to-day operations of the organization. Additional responsibilities include budgeting, customer service, fundraising, and managing seasonal staff and volunteers. The Executive Director reports to the Board of Directors, primarily to the Board President.

Requirements

A passion for non-motorized boating, outdoor education, and getting youth outdoors
Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience in Outdoor Education, Business Management, or similar disciplines
Strong leadership and team-building skills with the ability to manage seasonal employees and volunteers, and work with a Board of Directors
Ability to learn and utilize software to streamline booking, scheduling, accounting, and management processes
Able to pass a background check in accordance with our youth protection policy
Commitment to our mission and the value of boating education, safety, and environmental stewardship in defining our lives
Excellent communication and customer service skills
Strong analytical skills and attention to detail
Must be able to lift at least 50 pounds, work in outdoor environments, operate a computer and other office productivity machinery, perform maintenance on boats and other hard-to-access equipment, and possess a valid driver’s license

Compensation

This is a salaried position that requires 40 hours per week from June through September, then reducing to 10-12 hours per week during the rest of the year. Starting salary is $36,000 per year, but our Board is motivated to find the right leader to help us expand this to a year-round full-time position with a salary commensurate with fundraising performance.

How To Apply

The Oregon Boating Foundation is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

To apply, please submit your resume and a cover letter describing why you believe you’re a good fit for our organization to director@oregonboatingfoundation.org, or call 800-806-4882 for more information.

This position will remain open until filled.

jobs.title: Chief Program Officer, The AI Access Initiative
jobs.description:
1. About Our Client:
The AI Access Initiative (2AI) operates within the artificial intelligence sector, focusing on leveraging AI innovations to address poverty in low- and middle-income countries. The organization aims to bridge the development gap by scaling AI-enabled programs that benefit tens or hundreds of millions of people living in poverty. It collaborates with global development actors, AI labs, and researchers to create open-sourced resources and implement impactful AI solutions in agriculture and health, with outreach already impacting millions in countries such as India.

2. About the Opportunity:
The Chief Program Officer will lead and scale programmatic efforts across agriculture and health portfolios, playing a critical role in expanding AI-enabled interventions to reach tens of millions of beneficiaries. This position involves setting program strategy, building and managing teams, and establishing systems to ensure effective program delivery and learning. As a key leadership team member, the Chief Program Officer influences the organization’s direction and represents it to partners and funders.

3. Responsibilities:
• Serve on the executive leadership team, shaping strategy, culture, and priorities.
• Hire, mentor, and develop leadership and program staff.
• Establish rigorous learning architectures with clear hypotheses, measurement, iteration, and reporting.
• Guide resource allocation based on cost-effectiveness evidence.
• Develop and maintain open-sourced playbooks and insights.
• Set and execute ambitious program strategies and expansion plans.
• Collaborate closely with regional and program leaders.
• Build program teams, systems, and partnerships to support scaling.
• Engage with funders and external stakeholders as a key organizational representative.

4. Requirements:
• Experience leading large-scale program operations, preferably across multiple geographies.
• Strong ability to recruit and develop high-performing teams.
• Expertise in designing program strategy and organizational infrastructure.
• Skills in implementing learning and measurement processes.
• Ability to work collaboratively with diverse stakeholders including governments and partners.
• Experience in the global development, AI, health, or agriculture sectors is advantageous.

5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:
• The pay range and compensation package for this role will be determined based on the candidate’s experience, skills, and other relevant factors.

Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.

Note:
RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

jobs.title: Associate Director of Strategic Partnerships
jobs.description:
1. About Our Client:
The organization supports transformative Fellowships designed in partnership with leading corporations and foundations to aid entrepreneurs and leaders from underserved backgrounds. These Fellowships provide entrepreneurship and leadership training, mentorship, community stipends, long-term alumni support, and access to a global network of peers and opportunities. With partnerships involving major entities such as Google, JPMorganChase Foundation, Walmart.org, Wells Fargo, and others, the program reaches over 2,400 emerging leaders annually. Alumni have collectively raised over $190 million, created more than 2,300 paid jobs, and impacted over 11 million people globally, with a high rate of continued employment, education, or venture creation.

2. About the Opportunity:
The Associate Director of Strategic Partnerships is responsible for developing and expanding a portfolio of corporate and foundation partnerships that support the organization''s Fellowships. This role focuses on creating and maintaining strategic collaborations that align business and community impact goals, ensuring the growth and sustainability of the Fellowship programs.

3. Responsibilities:
• Build and grow partnerships with corporations and foundations to support Fellowship programs.
• Create tailored and persuasive impact updates including decks, emails, and profiles for prospects and partners.
• Lead and prepare for prospect and partner meetings, including managing follow-ups and coordinating with internal teams.
• Map and engage complex stakeholder networks within partner organizations.
• Develop concept notes, decks, and grant applications demonstrating both business and community impact.
• Design points of connection that align partnerships with corporate strategy and goals across multiple teams.
• Coordinate closely with senior leadership to achieve partnership objectives.
• Collaborate with the Design Team on monitoring, evaluation, and learning to meet partner expectations.
• Lead special projects critical to partnership strategy, such as market landscape analyses and thought leadership development.

4. Requirements:
• Strong attention to detail with excellent organizational and project management skills.
• Proven ability to create compelling, well-designed communications and presentations.
• Experience managing complex stakeholder engagement.
• Strategic thinking with the ability to design and implement effective systems and processes.
• High integrity and sound judgment in handling sensitive information and decision-making.
• Results-driven with a proactive and persistent approach to responsibilities.

5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:
• The pay range and compensation package for this role will be determined based on the candidate’s experience, skills, and other relevant factors.

Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.

Note:
RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

jobs.title: Managing Director of Project Finance, Energy Storage
jobs.description:
The Company

Our client is a rapidly growing developer and operator of utility scale energy storage projects, focused on deploying large battery systems to support grid reliability and renewable integration. The company is backed by institutional capital and is scaling a multi-market portfolio of battery energy storage assets.

Role Overview

Our client is seeking a Managing Director, Project Finance to join its leadership team at a critical stage of growth. This role is designed to expand senior leadership capacity within the project finance function, enabling the company to originate, structure, and execute multiple complex financings in parallel across its rapidly scaling energy storage portfolio. The Managing Director will operate as a senior leader within the team, working closely with existing leadership to drive transaction execution, capital formation, and broader financing strategy.

Duties & Responsibilities

Lead the origination, structuring, and execution of all project finance transactions across the company’s portfolio, including debt, tax equity, and structured equity financings
Take primary responsibility for financing strategy and execution across a large and growing pipeline of utility-scale BESS projects
Structure and negotiate complex financing solutions, including all commercial terms with lenders, tax equity investors, and counterparties
Lead all aspects of transaction execution, including financial modeling, diligence, structuring, tax analysis, documentation, approvals processes, and closing
Manage multiple transactions concurrently, ensuring high-quality execution across all internal and external workstreams
Build and maintain relationships with banks, tax equity providers, institutional investors, and financing partners
Lead and manage a team of project finance professionals, providing oversight, mentorship, and execution support
Coordinate closely with development, M&A, legal, accounting, engineering, and asset management teams to advance transactions
Support the economic evaluation of project acquisition and development opportunities, including valuation, structuring, and return analysis
Partner with the CFO and executive leadership on corporate-level financing activities, including platform equity raises, development capital, and LC / credit facilities
Prepare and present materials to internal stakeholders, including senior management, as well as external investors and lenders
Oversee diligence processes, third-party consultants, and transaction documentation across all financing activities
Ensure alignment across tax, accounting, legal, and technical workstreams throughout the transaction lifecycle
Contribute to the development and evolution of the company’s capital markets strategy, including new financing structures and approaches
Help build and scale the project finance function, including processes, infrastructure, and execution capabilities

Qualifications

10+ years of experience in renewable energy project finance, structured finance, or capital markets
Proven track record of executing complex project financings, including debt and tax equity
Deep expertise in tax equity structures, capital stacks, and transaction execution
Strong relationships with lenders, tax equity providers, and financial institutions
Experience leading transactions from origination through closing and approvals processes
Experience working across both project-level and corporate-level financing activities
Ability to operate at a senior, strategic level while remaining hands-on in execution
Experience managing or leading teams within a high-performing environment
Experience in a high-growth or scaling platform environment strongly preferred
Legally authorized to work in the U.S. without employer sponsorship
Ability to work in the office four days a week

Why Join

Step into a senior leadership role at a rapidly scaling energy storage platform
Direct influence on capital formation, financing strategy, and investment decisions
Exposure to both project-level and corporate-level financings
Ability to help build and scale a best-in-class project finance function

Show more

Show less

Employment type

Job function

Finance

Industries

Services for Renewable Energy and Utilities

jobs.title: Director of Operations
jobs.description:
What We Need

The Director of Operations holds a leadership responsibility for the operational performance across the company’s multinational manufacturing operations. This role oversees the strategic alignment of multiple plants to ensure excellence in quality, service, cost, compliance, and safety. As a leader, you will be responsible for developing regional talent, optimizing cross-site production flow, and ensuring regional revenue and profit targets are met. The Director is the primary architect of the multi-site strategic manufacturing plan, ensuring the region is positioned to meet both current and future business demands.

This is an onsite role based in our Milwaukee HQ office.

What You'll Be Doing

Operational Excellence: Standardize and maintain operating systems that drive repeatable processes and lead standard work across multiple locations, fostering a culture of continuous improvement and visual performance management.
Strategic Resource Planning: Lead end-to-end resource planning—including people, assets, space, and systems—across the US to achieve regional revenue, profit, and service targets.
Cross-Functional Partnership: Act as a fact-centered strategic partner for global functions (Finance, Supply Chain, Engineering), driving improvements that transcend individual plant boundaries.
Safety Leadership: Establish and scale a regional safety culture underpinned by reflexive leadership engagement and the systematic removal of risk-related behaviors across all sites.
Governance & Compliance: Ensure all company, regulatory, and customer-specific policies are integrated and followed by site leadership teams throughout the region.
Regional Roadmap Development: Create and execute an annual regional improvement plan that provides a credible roadmap for advancing safety, quality, delivery, and cost metrics.
Data-Driven Capability Building: Expand regional competency in data collection and rapid improvement cycles, ensuring sites can implement useful control systems and act on performance data.
Organizational Development: Serve as the primary owner for organizational health, ensuring employees understand work systems and that sites are adequately resourced to withstand leadership attrition.

What You Will Need To Be Successful

Education: Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Industrial, Mechanical or Manufacturing) or Operations Management from an accredited university or college. MBA with a focus on Operations Management or Global Management is preferred.
Experience: A minimum of 10 years (with at least 7 years of demonstrable career growth) in manufacturing leadership, ideally overseeing multiple sites.
Systems Proficiency: 5+ years of experience working with Tier 1 or Tier 2 ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics) in a manufacturing environment.
Multidisciplinary Background: Proven experience across multiple disciplines such as supply chain, sourcing, engineering, logistics, or finance.
Leadership: Extensive experience managing diverse teams, including both exempt-level plant management and nonexempt employees.
Communication: Proven strategic planning and communication skills, with the ability to influence at the executive level.
Analytical Skills: Proven ability to analyze metrics, financial reports, and performance data to effectively translate complex ERP data into actionable business insights for senior leadership.
Change Management: Proven experience leading teams through technology transitions or system-driven process improvements.
Travel: Ability to travel overnight up to 50%, both domestically and international.

About Us

Who we are:

Brady makes products that make the world a safer and more productive place. We are a global leader in safety, identification and compliance solutions for a diverse range of workplaces. From the depths of the ocean to outer space, from the factory floor to the delivery room - we're just about everywhere you look. Companies around the world trust Brady because of our deep expertise and knowledge across a wide range of industries and applications - powered by our world-class manufacturing capabilities.

We have a diverse customer base in industries including electronics, telecommunications, manufacturing, electrical, construction, healthcare, aerospace and more. As of July 31, 2025, Brady employed approximately 6,400 people in our worldwide businesses. Our fiscal 2025 sales were approximately $1.51 billion. Brady stock trades on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol BRC. You can learn more about us at www.bradycorp.com.

Why Work At Brady

A career at Brady means working for a global company that has thrived for over 100 years, and whose innovative spirit drives our future growth.

Brady offers competitive pay and great benefits, supported by a culture that encourages collaboration and innovation. We strive to foster an inclusive workplace where diverse talent can learn, grow, and succeed. And with deeply rooted values, no matter where you work at Brady, you'll feel connected to the community through our charitable contributions and opportunities to give back.

Our headquarters are in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, but we have more than 70 locations globally, giving our employees the opportunity to work with colleagues around the world.

Our Benefits

Comprehensive insurance coverage starting on your first day of employment, including medical, dental, and vision
Generous 401(k) with company match
Paid time off and holidays
Opportunity to participate in incentive programs for all full-time employees
Family planning benefits including paid parental leave, fertility coverage, adoption and surrogacy assistance
Education reimbursement opportunities
Scholarship program for children of Brady employees
A variety of Employee Resource Groups to provide you with unique networking, development, and volunteer opportunities
Employee Assistance Program and related wellness programs (mental and behavioral health, family counseling, financial management)
Dress-for-your-day dress code
Charitable contributions matched through Brady's Matching Gift program

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Management and Manufacturing

Industries

Manufacturing

jobs.title: Family Development Director
jobs.description:
At Upbring, our team is united by a shared mission: to break the cycle of child abuse and advance the wellbeing of children, families and communities, empowering them to thrive. As servant-leaders, we lead by example, working passionately to create a brighter future for those we serve. Every day, we strive to embody our core values:

We are Warriors: Fierce, resilient, and courageous, we face challenges head-on with unwavering loyalty and passion.
We are Servants: We dedicate ourselves to serving others, empowering those around us to thrive.
We are Family: We work together as a supportive, caring community, creating a sense of belonging and strength.

Joining Upbring means becoming part of a family of passionate individuals who are fearless in their pursuit of lasting change. Together, we are making a difference, one life at a time.

Your Mission in Action

The Family Development Director oversees the preservice process and implements best practices in family development. The Family Development Director will supervise the Upbring Kinship Verification team and the Upbring Pre-Service Verification Team for the 35/45 Corridor offices including Dallas, Fort Worth, Austin, San Antonio and Houston. This role collaborates with the Recruitment team, preservice families, and the Assistant Director (AD) to ensure efficiency and alignment across departments. The Family Development Director is also responsible for cultivating community partnerships and fostering strong engagement with preservice families throughout the development process.

Responsibilities

Supervise the Upbring Kinship Verification Team, statewide. Reviewing, processing and developing Kinship homes statewide
Supervise the Pre-Service Verification Team for the 35/45 Corridor including the Dallas, Fort Worth, Austin, San Antonio and Houston FIT offices. Assigning Regional Intake Specialist’s (RIS) to each In Process family and working them through the verification process
Supervise the Maintain Child Placement Management Staff requirements to conduct home screenings, assessments and reports ensuring they meet DFPS Minimum Standards, Contracting, YFT Guidelines, and agency requirements
Maintain knowledge of Upbring Foster in Texas (FIT) requirements for prospective foster/adoptive families; communicate requirements to families as needed
Conduct intake reviews and assess prospective foster and adoptive families, responding to inquiries, evaluating applications, and coordinating referrals to appropriate program offices to support the family licensing process
Manage a caseload of prospective foster and adoptive families, providing guidance and ongoing communication throughout the pre-service and license
Coordinate w ith the FIT Training Department to review and implement training requirements for in-process families and provide support when needed
Follow-up with writers and other contractors when items have not been received ensuring timelines are being maintained and tracked for families who are in the home study process
Travel within Texas for trainings, respite studies, home visits, recruitment and community events and other identified items or requests Assist staff with identifying and creating partnerships with other community agencies; establish and maintain relationships to aid in collaboration of recruitment and placement efforts
Coach and support staff to ensure they have the necessary tools and resources needed to optimize performance, ensure established program goals, and achieve operational excellence.
Communicate with direct reports in periodic one-on-one meetings
Organize and deliver regularly scheduled staff meetings to communicate present, on-going, and future updates of pertinent information, projects, and initiatives
Work closely with management team to interview, hire, and onboard new employees who are strong cultural fits for Upbring
Work in collaboration with leadership and the People and Culture Team to arrange training; when needed, coordinate and/or provide in-service training for staff development
Regularly review employee performance, including goals and progress, to provide developmental feedback: use applicable agency development tools to facilitate growth and development in staff
Draft and issue the 90-day and annual evaluations, along with supporting documentation, as per established procedures; work in collaboration with staff to set goals during evaluation periods

Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in social work, counseling, psychology, social services, or closely related field
Child Placement Management Staff (CPMS)
Three (3) years’ relevant experience in foster care, child welfare, or adoption (or six months’ experience if a current Upbring employee)
Strong organizational and time management skills with the ability to meet deadlines and prioritize competing tasks/projects
Strong people and interpersonal skills with the ability to interact and work with diverse groups of people
Proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel, and Outlook

Preferred Qualifications

Master’s degree in social work, counseling, psychology, social services, or closely related field
Licensed Child Agency Administrator or eligible within 12 months of hire
Bilingual (Spanish) proficiency ( required for certain areas based on populations serve)

Travel

This position may be required to travel up to 20%

Perks at Upbring

Competitive PTO & paid holidays 
Health, dental, vision insurance & more 
403(b) Plan
Employee Assistance Program
Discounted Gym Memberships 

Physical Demands & Work Conditions

This position requires sitting and looking and using a computer for long periods of time
Position works in a child facility and has constant exposure to children
Position requires frequent standing and walking
Lift, push, pull, move up to 25 lbs.

The physical requirements for this position with or without reasonable accommodation are representative of those that must be met by an employee to perform the essential functions of this position successfully. While performing this position the employee with or without reasonable accommodation should have the ability to walk, stand, sit, kneel, speak, and hear with aid, see, read, reach with arms and hands, lift and/or move up to specified weight, and use equipment that is essential to performing the job, such as a computer, copier, fax machine, telephone, and automobile, if applicable. Specific vision requirements of this position may include color vision, seeing things close up, and the ability to adjust focus.

Upbring is an Equal Employment Opportunity/AA Employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, ancestry, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender, pregnancy, marital status, national origin, genetic information, physical or mental disability, military or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.

Someone who understands clinical trial processes inside out, has the network to open doors, and the drive to close them. This person should be able to work with full autonomy - owning their territory, generating their own pipeline, and representing Biorce at the highest levels of the industry.
Key Responsibilities

Own and drive the full sales cycle, from lead generation to deal closure with pharmaceutical and biotech clients across the East Coast USA.
Responsibility for hitting quarterly targets and consistently delivering against ambitious commercial goals.
Develop and execute go-to-market strategies tailored to the U.S. market, in collaboration with the Chief Commercial Officer and Global head of commercial operations.
Identify high-value potential clients and decision makers, and cultivate strong, trust based relationships.
Represent Biorce at industry events, conferences, and client meetings.
Collaborate closely with Marketing, Clinical Solutions, and Product teams to tailor pitches and ensure client success.
Provide market intelligence and feedback to inform product development and strategic decisions.
Help define and refine commercial processes and infrastructure as we scale operations in the U.S.
Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively across teams, you take full ownership of your objectives, responsibilities, and assigned territory with professionalism and drive.

Requirements

✅ Must-haves
5+ years of experience in business development, strategic partnerships, or enterprise sales in the biotech, pharmaceutical, or CRO sectors.
Deep understanding of clinical trial processes and a strong network in the U.S. biopharma ecosystem.
Proven track record of closing mid-to-large enterprise deals and building long-term relationships with C-level stakeholders.
Entrepreneurial mindset with the ability to operate independently in a fast-paced, startup environment.
Strong communication and presentation skills
Ability to explain complex technology in simple, value driven terms.
Willingness to travel up to 50% across the US and internationally to company offsites.

✨ Nice-to-Haves
Familiarity with AI-driven platforms in a clinical or drug development context

What We Offer

Opportunity to shape the future of a fast-growing AI healthtech company entering the U.S. market.
Opportunity to grow into broader strategic role as Biorce expands.
US (New York / Austin): $150,000 - $200,000

This role is commission-eligible. Final compensation depends on experience, skills, and location. The range above reflects our good-faith estimate at the time of posting, in line with Labor Law and similar pay transparency regulations.

A dynamic work environment with an international team, where collaboration and diversity thrive

Comprehensive private health coverage to ensure your physical and mental well-being
Hybrid work model offering flexibility to balance your professional and personal life
Company events to celebrate achievements and enjoy time together
Get equipped with a MacBook to enhance your productivity and work experience

new_jobs.title: Executive Director
new_jobs.description:
The Oregon Boating Foundation, a 501(c)(3) charitable organization located in Newport, Oregon, is looking for an Executive Director with a passion for kayaking, sailing, and outdoor education. The ideal candidate is an experienced leader with excellent verbal and written communication skills that will represent our organization publicly, support a culture of longevity, and grow the capacity of the organization.

This is a unique opportunity for the right person to advance their career, positively impact our organization on a daily basis, and grow our programs.

Job Description

The Executive Director contributes to the Oregon Boating Foundation’s mission of making safe, affordable boating opportunities available to everyone by managing the day-to-day operations of the organization. Additional responsibilities include budgeting, customer service, fundraising, and managing seasonal staff and volunteers. The Executive Director reports to the Board of Directors, primarily to the Board President.

Requirements

A passion for non-motorized boating, outdoor education, and getting youth outdoors
Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience in Outdoor Education, Business Management, or similar disciplines
Strong leadership and team-building skills with the ability to manage seasonal employees and volunteers, and work with a Board of Directors
Ability to learn and utilize software to streamline booking, scheduling, accounting, and management processes
Able to pass a background check in accordance with our youth protection policy
Commitment to our mission and the value of boating education, safety, and environmental stewardship in defining our lives
Excellent communication and customer service skills
Strong analytical skills and attention to detail
Must be able to lift at least 50 pounds, work in outdoor environments, operate a computer and other office productivity machinery, perform maintenance on boats and other hard-to-access equipment, and possess a valid driver’s license

Compensation

This is a salaried position that requires 40 hours per week from June through September, then reducing to 10-12 hours per week during the rest of the year. Starting salary is $36,000 per year, but our Board is motivated to find the right leader to help us expand this to a year-round full-time position with a salary commensurate with fundraising performance.

How To Apply

The Oregon Boating Foundation is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

To apply, please submit your resume and a cover letter describing why you believe you’re a good fit for our organization to director@oregonboatingfoundation.org, or call 800-806-4882 for more information.

This position will remain open until filled.

new_jobs.title: Chief Program Officer, The AI Access Initiative
new_jobs.description:
1. About Our Client:
The AI Access Initiative (2AI) operates within the artificial intelligence sector, focusing on leveraging AI innovations to address poverty in low- and middle-income countries. The organization aims to bridge the development gap by scaling AI-enabled programs that benefit tens or hundreds of millions of people living in poverty. It collaborates with global development actors, AI labs, and researchers to create open-sourced resources and implement impactful AI solutions in agriculture and health, with outreach already impacting millions in countries such as India.

2. About the Opportunity:
The Chief Program Officer will lead and scale programmatic efforts across agriculture and health portfolios, playing a critical role in expanding AI-enabled interventions to reach tens of millions of beneficiaries. This position involves setting program strategy, building and managing teams, and establishing systems to ensure effective program delivery and learning. As a key leadership team member, the Chief Program Officer influences the organization’s direction and represents it to partners and funders.

3. Responsibilities:
• Serve on the executive leadership team, shaping strategy, culture, and priorities.
• Hire, mentor, and develop leadership and program staff.
• Establish rigorous learning architectures with clear hypotheses, measurement, iteration, and reporting.
• Guide resource allocation based on cost-effectiveness evidence.
• Develop and maintain open-sourced playbooks and insights.
• Set and execute ambitious program strategies and expansion plans.
• Collaborate closely with regional and program leaders.
• Build program teams, systems, and partnerships to support scaling.
• Engage with funders and external stakeholders as a key organizational representative.

4. Requirements:
• Experience leading large-scale program operations, preferably across multiple geographies.
• Strong ability to recruit and develop high-performing teams.
• Expertise in designing program strategy and organizational infrastructure.
• Skills in implementing learning and measurement processes.
• Ability to work collaboratively with diverse stakeholders including governments and partners.
• Experience in the global development, AI, health, or agriculture sectors is advantageous.

5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:
• The pay range and compensation package for this role will be determined based on the candidate’s experience, skills, and other relevant factors.

Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.

Note:

We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

new_jobs.title: Associate Director of Strategic Partnerships
new_jobs.description:
1. About Our Client:
The organization supports transformative Fellowships designed in partnership with leading corporations and foundations to aid entrepreneurs and leaders from underserved backgrounds. These Fellowships provide entrepreneurship and leadership training, mentorship, community stipends, long-term alumni support, and access to a global network of peers and opportunities. With partnerships involving major entities such as Google, JPMorganChase Foundation, Walmart.org, Wells Fargo, and others, the program reaches over 2,400 emerging leaders annually. Alumni have collectively raised over $190 million, created more than 2,300 paid jobs, and impacted over 11 million people globally, with a high rate of continued employment, education, or venture creation.

2. About the Opportunity:
The Associate Director of Strategic Partnerships is responsible for developing and expanding a portfolio of corporate and foundation partnerships that support the organization''s Fellowships. This role focuses on creating and maintaining strategic collaborations that align business and community impact goals, ensuring the growth and sustainability of the Fellowship programs.

3. Responsibilities:
• Build and grow partnerships with corporations and foundations to support Fellowship programs.
• Create tailored and persuasive impact updates including decks, emails, and profiles for prospects and partners.
• Lead and prepare for prospect and partner meetings, including managing follow-ups and coordinating with internal teams.
• Map and engage complex stakeholder networks within partner organizations.
• Develop concept notes, decks, and grant applications demonstrating both business and community impact.
• Design points of connection that align partnerships with corporate strategy and goals across multiple teams.
• Coordinate closely with senior leadership to achieve partnership objectives.
• Collaborate with the Design Team on monitoring, evaluation, and learning to meet partner expectations.
• Lead special projects critical to partnership strategy, such as market landscape analyses and thought leadership development.

4. Requirements:
• Strong attention to detail with excellent organizational and project management skills.
• Proven ability to create compelling, well-designed communications and presentations.
• Experience managing complex stakeholder engagement.
• Strategic thinking with the ability to design and implement effective systems and processes.
• High integrity and sound judgment in handling sensitive information and decision-making.
• Results-driven with a proactive and persistent approach to responsibilities.

5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

new_jobs.title: Managing Director of Project Finance, Energy Storage
new_jobs.description:
The Company

Our client is a rapidly growing developer and operator of utility scale energy storage projects, focused on deploying large battery systems to support grid reliability and renewable integration. The company is backed by institutional capital and is scaling a multi-market portfolio of battery energy storage assets.

Role Overview

Our client is seeking a Managing Director, Project Finance to join its leadership team at a critical stage of growth. This role is designed to expand senior leadership capacity within the project finance function, enabling the company to originate, structure, and execute multiple complex financings in parallel across its rapidly scaling energy storage portfolio. The Managing Director will operate as a senior leader within the team, working closely with existing leadership to drive transaction execution, capital formation, and broader financing strategy.

Duties & Responsibilities

Lead the origination, structuring, and execution of all project finance transactions across the company’s portfolio, including debt, tax equity, and structured equity financings
Take primary responsibility for financing strategy and execution across a large and growing pipeline of utility-scale BESS projects
Structure and negotiate complex financing solutions, including all commercial terms with lenders, tax equity investors, and counterparties
Lead all aspects of transaction execution, including financial modeling, diligence, structuring, tax analysis, documentation, approvals processes, and closing
Manage multiple transactions concurrently, ensuring high-quality execution across all internal and external workstreams
Build and maintain relationships with banks, tax equity providers, institutional investors, and financing partners
Lead and manage a team of project finance professionals, providing oversight, mentorship, and execution support
Coordinate closely with development, M&A, legal, accounting, engineering, and asset management teams to advance transactions
Support the economic evaluation of project acquisition and development opportunities, including valuation, structuring, and return analysis
Partner with the CFO and executive leadership on corporate-level financing activities, including platform equity raises, development capital, and LC / credit facilities
Prepare and present materials to internal stakeholders, including senior management, as well as external investors and lenders
Oversee diligence processes, third-party consultants, and transaction documentation across all financing activities
Ensure alignment across tax, accounting, legal, and technical workstreams throughout the transaction lifecycle
Contribute to the development and evolution of the company’s capital markets strategy, including new financing structures and approaches
Help build and scale the project finance function, including processes, infrastructure, and execution capabilities

Qualifications

10+ years of experience in renewable energy project finance, structured finance, or capital markets
Proven track record of executing complex project financings, including debt and tax equity
Deep expertise in tax equity structures, capital stacks, and transaction execution
Strong relationships with lenders, tax equity providers, and financial institutions
Experience leading transactions from origination through closing and approvals processes
Experience working across both project-level and corporate-level financing activities
Ability to operate at a senior, strategic level while remaining hands-on in execution
Experience managing or leading teams within a high-performing environment
Experience in a high-growth or scaling platform environment strongly preferred
Legally authorized to work in the U.S. without employer sponsorship
Ability to work in the office four days a week

Why Join

Step into a senior leadership role at a rapidly scaling energy storage platform
Direct influence on capital formation, financing strategy, and investment decisions
Exposure to both project-level and corporate-level financings
Ability to help build and scale a best-in-class project finance function

new_jobs.title: Business Development Director - US
new_jobs.description:
About the company
Biorce is a pioneering Healthtech company dedicated to revolutionizing drug development through the power of AI. We are passionate about accelerating medical advancements and improving patient outcomes.
Our team comprises seasoned clinical research professionals, data scientists, and AI experts, working collaboratively to bridge the gap between cutting-edge technology and real-world clinical needs.
With an unwavering commitment to revolutionize healthcare, we envision a world where all patients benefit from accelerated and cost-effective access to treatments. Biorce is poised to redefine the landscape of healthcare, shaping a future where innovation and accessibility converge for the betterment of humanity.

About the role
Following our successful expansion into the U.S. and continued growth across Europe, we are seeking a Director of Clinical Solutions
to help drive our commercial function from one of our U.S. hubs ( New York or Austin ).
As our Director of Clinical Solutions, you’ll be a critical member of our commercial team, responsible for driving growth, building relationships with biopharma partners, and positioning Biorce as a category leader in AI for clinical development. This is a high-impact role that combines strategy, sales, and client relationship management, with significant opportunity for career growth as our U.S. footprint expands.
Who We're Looking For

A commercially sharp operator with deep credibility in the U.S. biopharma space. Someone who understands clinical trial processes inside out, has the network to open doors, and the drive to close them. This person should be able to work with full autonomy - owning their territory, generating their own pipeline, and representing Biorce at the highest levels of the industry.
Key Responsibilities

Own and drive the full sales cycle, from lead generation to deal closure with pharmaceutical and biotech clients across the East Coast USA.

Represent Biorce at industry events, conferences, and client meetings.
Collaborate closely with Marketing, Clinical Solutions, and Product teams to tailor pitches and ensure client success.
Provide market intelligence and feedback to inform product development and strategic decisions.
Help define and refine commercial processes and infrastructure as we scale operations in the U.S.
Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively across teams, you take full ownership of your objectives, responsibilities, and assigned territory with professionalism and drive.

Requirements

✅ Must-haves
5+ years of experience in business development, strategic partnerships, or enterprise sales in the biotech, pharmaceutical, or CRO sectors.
Deep understanding of clinical trial processes and a strong network in the U.S. biopharma ecosystem.
Proven track record of closing mid-to-large enterprise deals and building long-term relationships with C-level stakeholders.
Entrepreneurial mindset with the ability to operate independently in a fast-paced, startup environment.

new_jobs.title: Director of Operations
new_jobs.description:
What We Need

The Director of Operations holds a leadership responsibility for the operational performance across the company’s multinational manufacturing operations. This role oversees the strategic alignment of multiple plants to ensure excellence in quality, service, cost, compliance, and safety. As a leader, you will be responsible for developing regional talent, optimizing cross-site production flow, and ensuring regional revenue and profit targets are met. The Director is the primary architect of the multi-site strategic manufacturing plan, ensuring the region is positioned to meet both current and future business demands.

This is an onsite role based in our Milwaukee HQ office.

What You'll Be Doing

Operational Excellence: Standardize and maintain operating systems that drive repeatable processes and lead standard work across multiple locations, fostering a culture of continuous improvement and visual performance management.
Strategic Resource Planning: Lead end-to-end resource planning—including people, assets, space, and systems—across the US to achieve regional revenue, profit, and service targets.
Cross-Functional Partnership: Act as a fact-centered strategic partner for global functions (Finance, Supply Chain, Engineering), driving improvements that transcend individual plant boundaries.
Safety Leadership: Establish and scale a regional safety culture underpinned by reflexive leadership engagement and the systematic removal of risk-related behaviors across all sites.
Governance & Compliance: Ensure all company, regulatory, and customer-specific policies are integrated and followed by site leadership teams throughout the region.
Regional Roadmap Development: Create and execute an annual regional improvement plan that provides a credible roadmap for advancing safety, quality, delivery, and cost metrics.
Data-Driven Capability Building: Expand regional competency in data collection and rapid improvement cycles, ensuring sites can implement useful control systems and act on performance data.
Organizational Development: Serve as the primary owner for organizational health, ensuring employees understand work systems and that sites are adequately resourced to withstand leadership attrition.

What You Will Need To Be Successful

Education: Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Industrial, Mechanical or Manufacturing) or Operations Management from an accredited university or college. MBA with a focus on Operations Management or Global Management is preferred.
Experience: A minimum of 10 years (with at least 7 years of demonstrable career growth) in manufacturing leadership, ideally overseeing multiple sites.
Systems Proficiency: 5+ years of experience working with Tier 1 or Tier 2 ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics) in a manufacturing environment.
Multidisciplinary Background: Proven experience across multiple disciplines such as supply chain, sourcing, engineering, logistics, or finance.
Leadership: Extensive experience managing diverse teams, including both exempt-level plant management and nonexempt employees.
Communication: Proven strategic planning and communication skills, with the ability to influence at the executive level.
Analytical Skills: Proven ability to analyze metrics, financial reports, and performance data to effectively translate complex ERP data into actionable business insights for senior leadership.
Change Management: Proven experience leading teams through technology transitions or system-driven process improvements.
Travel: Ability to travel overnight up to 50%, both domestically and international.

About Us

Who we are:

Brady makes products that make the world a safer and more productive place. We are a global leader in safety, identification and compliance solutions for a diverse range of workplaces. From the depths of the ocean to outer space, from the factory floor to the delivery room - we're just about everywhere you look. Companies around the world trust Brady because of our deep expertise and knowledge across a wide range of industries and applications - powered by our world-class manufacturing capabilities.

We have a diverse customer base in industries including electronics, telecommunications, manufacturing, electrical, construction, healthcare, aerospace and more. As of July 31, 2025, Brady employed approximately 6,400 people in our worldwide businesses. Our fiscal 2025 sales were approximately $1.51 billion. Brady stock trades on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol BRC. You can learn more about us at www.bradycorp.com.

Why Work At Brady

A career at Brady means working for a global company that has thrived for over 100 years, and whose innovative spirit drives our future growth.

Brady offers competitive pay and great benefits, supported by a culture that encourages collaboration and innovation. We strive to foster an inclusive workplace where diverse talent can learn, grow, and succeed. And with deeply rooted values, no matter where you work at Brady, you'll feel connected to the community through our charitable contributions and opportunities to give back.

Our headquarters are in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, but we have more than 70 locations globally, giving our employees the opportunity to work with colleagues around the world.

Our Benefits

Comprehensive insurance coverage starting on your first day of employment, including medical, dental, and vision
Generous 401(k) with company match
Paid time off and holidays
Opportunity to participate in incentive programs for all full-time employees
Family planning benefits including paid parental leave, fertility coverage, adoption and surrogacy assistance
Education reimbursement opportunities
Scholarship program for children of Brady employees
A variety of Employee Resource Groups to provide you with unique networking, development, and volunteer opportunities
Employee Assistance Program and related wellness programs (mental and behavioral health, family counseling, financial management)
Dress-for-your-day dress code
Charitable contributions matched through Brady's Matching Gift program

new_jobs.title: Family Development Director
new_jobs.description:
At Upbring, our team is united by a shared mission: to break the cycle of child abuse and advance the wellbeing of children, families and communities, empowering them to thrive. As servant-leaders, we lead by example, working passionately to create a brighter future for those we serve. Every day, we strive to embody our core values:

We are Warriors: Fierce, resilient, and courageous, we face challenges head-on with unwavering loyalty and passion.
We are Servants: We dedicate ourselves to serving others, empowering those around us to thrive.
We are Family: We work together as a supportive, caring community, creating a sense of belonging and strength.

Joining Upbring means becoming part of a family of passionate individuals who are fearless in their pursuit of lasting change. Together, we are making a difference, one life at a time.

Your Mission in Action

The Family Development Director oversees the preservice process and implements best practices in family development. The Family Development Director will supervise the Upbring Kinship Verification team and the Upbring Pre-Service Verification Team for the 35/45 Corridor offices including Dallas, Fort Worth, Austin, San Antonio and Houston. This role collaborates with the Recruitment team, preservice families, and the Assistant Director (AD) to ensure efficiency and alignment across departments. The Family Development Director is also responsible for cultivating community partnerships and fostering strong engagement with preservice families throughout the development process.

Responsibilities

Supervise the Upbring Kinship Verification Team, statewide. Reviewing, processing and developing Kinship homes statewide
Supervise the Pre-Service Verification Team for the 35/45 Corridor including the Dallas, Fort Worth, Austin, San Antonio and Houston FIT offices. Assigning Regional Intake Specialist’s (RIS) to each In Process family and working them through the verification process
Supervise the Maintain Child Placement Management Staff requirements to conduct home screenings, assessments and reports ensuring they meet DFPS Minimum Standards, Contracting, YFT Guidelines, and agency requirements
Maintain knowledge of Upbring Foster in Texas (FIT) requirements for prospective foster/adoptive families; communicate requirements to families as needed
Conduct intake reviews and assess prospective foster and adoptive families, responding to inquiries, evaluating applications, and coordinating referrals to appropriate program offices to support the family licensing process
Manage a caseload of prospective foster and adoptive families, providing guidance and ongoing communication throughout the pre-service and license
Coordinate w ith the FIT Training Department to review and implement training requirements for in-process families and provide support when needed
Follow-up with writers and other contractors when items have not been received ensuring timelines are being maintained and tracked for families who are in the home study process
Travel within Texas for trainings, respite studies, home visits, recruitment and community events and other identified items or requests Assist staff with identifying and creating partnerships with other community agencies; establish and maintain relationships to aid in collaboration of recruitment and placement efforts
Coach and support staff to ensure they have the necessary tools and resources needed to optimize performance, ensure established program goals, and achieve operational excellence.
Communicate with direct reports in periodic one-on-one meetings
Organize and deliver regularly scheduled staff meetings to communicate present, on-going, and future updates of pertinent information, projects, and initiatives
Work closely with management team to interview, hire, and onboard new employees who are strong cultural fits for Upbring
Work in collaboration with leadership and the People and Culture Team to arrange training; when needed, coordinate and/or provide in-service training for staff development
Regularly review employee performance, including goals and progress, to provide developmental feedback: use applicable agency development tools to facilitate growth and development in staff
Draft and issue the 90-day and annual evaluations, along with supporting documentation, as per established procedures; work in collaboration with staff to set goals during evaluation periods

Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in social work, counseling, psychology, social services, or closely related field
Child Placement Management Staff (CPMS)
Three (3) years’ relevant experience in foster care, child welfare, or adoption (or six months’ experience if a current Upbring employee)
Strong organizational and time management skills with the ability to meet deadlines and prioritize competing tasks/projects
Strong people and interpersonal skills with the ability to interact and work with diverse groups of people
Proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel, and Outlook

Preferred Qualifications

Master’s degree in social work, counseling, psychology, social services, or closely related field
Licensed Child Agency Administrator or eligible within 12 months of hire
Bilingual (Spanish) proficiency ( required for certain areas based on populations serve)

Travel

This position may be required to travel up to 20%

Perks at Upbring

Competitive PTO & paid holidays 
Health, dental, vision insurance & more 
403(b) Plan
Employee Assistance Program
Discounted Gym Memberships 

Physical Demands & Work Conditions

This position requires sitting and looking and using a computer for long periods of time
Position works in a child facility and has constant exposure to children
Position requires frequent standing and walking
Lift, push, pull, move up to 25 lbs.

The physical requirements for this position with or without reasonable accommodation are representative of those that must be met by an employee to perform the essential functions of this position successfully. While performing this position the employee with or without reasonable accommodation should have the ability to walk, stand, sit, kneel, speak, and hear with aid, see, read, reach with arms and hands, lift and/or move up to specified weight, and use equipment that is essential to performing the job, such as a computer, copier, fax machine, telephone, and automobile, if applicable. Specific vision requirements of this position may include color vision, seeing things close up, and the ability to adjust focus.

Upbring is an Equal Employment Opportunity/AA Employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, ancestry, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender, pregnancy, marital status, national origin, genetic information, physical or mental disability, military or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.
